name: Druid Circle
description: Ancient keepers of the old growth. Patient, unhurried, connected to living things in ways outsiders never quite understand. They do not conquer land — they tend it. Their missions feel like stewardship: protecting what exists, restoring what was lost.
flavorNote: They call you the Hearthkeeper. Your fire is the only fire they trust.

name: Dwarven Company
description: A tight unit of mountain dwarves who have worked together long enough that words are mostly unnecessary. Gruff, loyal, direct. They will not complain about a hard march or a bad night, but they will notice — and remember — who kept them fed.
flavorNote: They call you the Provender. Among dwarves, that is high praise.

name: Corsair Fleet
description: Free sailors who answer to no flag and no crown. Chaotic, adaptable, willing to try anything once. Their world is salt air and opportunity. They eat what they can get and go where the money is — but they have learned that the right meal before a hard job changes everything.
flavorNote: They call you the Galley Witch. They mean it as a compliment.

name: The Greycloaks
description: Wandering wardens who answer to no crown and hold no land. They have been at the edges of civilization long enough that the edges feel like home. They know where the threats are before anyone else does, and they move through the world as though they belong to it in a way others do not.
flavorNote: They don't give you a name. Among the Greycloaks, trusting someone with your provisions is enough.
